meaning: n.[台球]连撞两球,连击,<美>碰击弹回vi.一击连撞二球,碰击而反跳
examples: To make a carom, as in billiards.
Honestly, I play more pool nowadays then carom.
I mean, who is going to beat them in carom or in pool?
He was thrown out at second off the carom, and replays showed the usher barely made it out of the way.
Christian Ehrhoff scored the lone goal, 17 seconds into the third period, when his fortunate carom off the end boards sneaked in behind Steve Mason.
